# Code of Conduct

I want this repo to feel straightforward and technically serious without turning hostile or weird.

## Expectations

- Be respectful, direct, and technically specific.
- Assume good intent and critique code, not people.
- Keep discussions focused on the project and its users.
- Do not harass, intimidate, or discriminate against participants.

## Enforcement

Project maintainers may remove comments, issues, pull requests, or access that violate these expectations.

## Reporting

Please report serious conduct problems privately to the maintainers rather than escalating them in public threads.
